subject: History
chapter_name: Tribals, Dikus and the Vision of a Golden Age
overall_learning_objectives: Learn the meaning of the term Diku
Identify problems faced by tribals during British rule
Understand the role of Birsa Munda
Explain the idea of the Vision of a Golden Age

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Tribal Communities
student_activities: Discuss how tribals depend on forests

lesson_plan.title: Tribal Life and Occupations
student_activities: Write two ways tribals used forests

lesson_plan.title: Who Were the Dikus?
student_activities: List examples of dikus such as traders and moneylenders

lesson_plan.title: Birsa Munda and Tribal Resistance
topics_covered: His role as a tribal leader
student_activities: Write three points about Birsa Munda
Create a small timeline of his movement

lesson_plan.title: Vision of a Golden Age
topics_covered: Birsa’s idea of a Golden Age
student_activities: Explain the meaning of the Golden Age
Write why tribals supported Birsa Munda

lesson_plan.title: Tribal Revolts and Chapter Revision
student_activities: List two causes of tribal revolts

teacher_tip: Use stories about tribal leaders and examples from tribal life to help students understand the struggles and culture of tribal communities.
